summary: Decompiled the Snap menu main per-frame update function fn_802545C4 to a 99.373% match. The function is a large state-machine switch covering states 0–23 for slot selection, photo browsing, dialog handling, and copy/move/delete card operations. The PR also adjusted nearby Snap menu helpers and fixed two mnsnap.h prototypes: mnSnap_8025409C now takes an s32 dialog type instead of an HSD_JObj* placeholder, and mnSnap_8025441C has its return type corrected. Review focused on avoiding unnecessary explicit assert/string data hacks and using normal HSD_ASSERT / inline constants instead.
what_changed: Implemented/decompiled fn_802545C4, the Snap menu's main per-frame update routine, as a large switch over mnSnap_804A0A10.state.
Covered Snap menu behavior including intro countdown, slot/card selection, photo browsing, dialog confirmations, dialog animation/timer updates, and copy/move/delete card operation flows.

Added a small static inline helper mnSnap_InitDialogText to centralize repeated dialog text creation and configuration through HSD_SisLib_803A5ACC, default_alignment, default_kerning, and font_size setup.
Changed button input storage in fn_802545C4 to u64 buttons, matching the call pattern around mn_80229624(4) and mn_804A04F0.buttons.
Used direct accesses to global mnSnap_804A0A10 in the matching version rather than consistently relying on a local mnSnap_State* alias, likely because it improved code generation for this function.
Inserted PAD_STACK(336) in fn_802545C4 as an explicit stack-layout matching tactic.
Added #pragma dont_inline around mnSnap_80254014 to prevent unwanted inlining and preserve matching behavior.
Corrected mnSnap_8025409C from taking an HSD_JObj* placeholder argument to taking s32 dlg_type, with comparisons updated from pointer/null style to integer dialog type checks.
Corrected mnSnap_8025441C's prototype in mnsnap.h according to the PR context; exact old/new return types are not visible in the excerpt.
Kept or restored HSD_SisLib_803A6B98 format strings such as "%03d" and "%d" as inline constants instead of promoting them to explicit local variables/SDATA entries.
smart_moves: Factored repeated dialog text initialization into mnSnap_InitDialogText, reducing duplicated code while preserving the specific HSD_Text field writes needed for matching.
Used a typed s32 dlg_type for mnSnap_8025409C, replacing a misleading HSD_JObj* parameter that was being cast/compared as an integer dialog mode.
Applied PAD_STACK(336) and #pragma dont_inline selectively as matching levers instead of rewriting large parts of the state machine.
Preserved the large switch/state-machine structure rather than hiding behavior behind high-level abstractions, making the decompiled control flow easier to compare to assembly.
Responded to review by reverting explicit assert/string-data hacks that were considered unnecessary.
decomp_lessons: For decompilation PRs, avoid turning implicit assert strings or format literals into explicit SDATA/static variables unless there is clear evidence the original binary uses separately addressable objects.
Use HSD_ASSERT when the existing source pattern implies implicit strings; manually expanding to __assert with custom string symbols can be an unnecessary and review-rejected hack.
If a parameter is semantically an integer mode, fix the prototype to s32 rather than preserving a pointer type only because the old undecompiled stub used casts.
Large menu update functions may require direct global-state accesses, local scoped temporaries, and explicit stack padding to approach a match.

Small inline helpers can be useful when they reproduce a repeated instruction pattern, but comments claiming a pattern from another file should be evidence-backed; reviewers may question unexplained local hacks.
Format strings passed directly to HSD_SisLib calls may be inline constants in source rather than separate named variables; do not overfit data symbols from assembly output.
naming_conventions: Unidentified Snap menu functions continue to use address-style names such as fn_802545C4 and mnSnap_8025409C.
The global Snap menu state object is named mnSnap_804A0A10.
Semantic local/state names in the decompiled function include active_slot, card_status, photo_count, pending_loads, dlg_active, dlg_timer, dlg_type, btn_idx, and other_slot.
Helper name mnSnap_InitDialogText follows project style by adding a descriptive suffix after the mnSnap module prefix for a newly introduced static inline.
HSD object pointers retain conventional names such as HSD_JObj, jobj, dlg_frame, dlg_pos, slot_a_jobj, slot_b_jobj, fullview_jobj, yes_jobj, and no_jobj.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(336) in fn_802545C4 to influence stack frame layout.
Used #pragma dont_inline on mnSnap_80254014 to prevent the compiler from inlining a helper that needed to remain separate for matching.
Used direct mnSnap_804A0A10 global accesses and scoped temporary variables such as cs0/cs/other_slot to shape generated code.
Used byte-offset pointer arithmetic to access JObj fields in mnSnap_804A0A10, e.g. selecting slot jobj pointers through offsets around 0x98.
Kept explicit HSD_JObjReqAnim/HSD_JObjAnim sequences around slot and dialog animations, including assert checks for dlg_pos before reading translate.x.
The final match remained partial at 99.373%, so the tactics were effective but did not fully eliminate mismatch.

review_feedback.author: jellejurre
review_feedback.feedback: Explicit __assert expansions should be reverted; use HSD_ASSERT with implicit strings instead.

review_feedback.author: jellejurre
review_feedback.feedback: The same assert cleanup applies to repeated JObj/image assert chains.

review_feedback.author: jellejurre
review_feedback.feedback: Likely inline constants such as format strings should not be treated as inlined variables or separate data symbols.

review_feedback.author: jellejurre
review_feedback.feedback: Questioned why added SDATA/static string declarations and the local inline hack were needed.

review_feedback.author: malvarezcastillo
review_feedback.feedback: Author acknowledged the issue and reverted the unnecessary hack.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish matching fn_802545C4 or improve the remaining 0.627% mismatch.
Search for other mnsnap PRs that replace address-style names with semantic names for Snap menu state handlers.
Look for project guidance or PRs about when to use HSD_ASSERT versus explicit __assert in decomp source.
Search for similar menu state-machine decompilations that use PAD_STACK and direct global-state accesses.
Inspect later changes to mnSnap_8025409C and mnSnap_8025441C prototypes for downstream type cleanup.
